handoff.root_cause_summary: Mandatory artifacts were missing and the defect was detected downstream.
handoff.repository_gap_hypothesis: A repeatable repository control may be weak.
handoff.recurrence_risk: repeatable if the same control remains weak
handoff.repository_maintenance_scope: verify and patch only the smallest evidence-backed repository gap
handoff.standalone_repair_prompt:
[ROLE]
Bounded repository-maintenance implementer and independent root-cause verifier. Do not assume access to the original Architect conversation. The active Architect Run must not edit repository files or create a PR.
[TARGET REPOSITORY]
repository: rezahh107/EV4-Architect-Repo
default_branch: main
observed_revision: [LIVE_HEAD_TO_REVALIDATE]
[OBSERVED INCIDENT]
incident_id: P01
Mandatory artifacts were missing and the defect was detected downstream.
[CURRENT-RUN EVIDENCE]
first_broken_stage: /score-evidence
first_detection_stage: /score-audit
current_run_status: repaired
resume_stage: /score-evidence
evidence: external trace, observed invalidation
[SUSPECTED REPOSITORY GAP]
state: probable
class: fail_late_detection
hypothesis: A repeatable repository control may be weak.
The incident description is evidence to investigate, not an authoritative diagnosis.
The repository-maintenance model must verify or reject the hypothesis from live repository evidence.
[UNCERTAINTIES]
exact affected Validator path: [AFFECTED_VALIDATOR_OR_CONTRACT]
Unknowns remain unknown; do not invent SHAs, paths, PRs, schemas, tests, or results.
[REQUIRED LIVE REPOSITORY REVIEW]
Revalidate the live default branch and exact current Head. Inspect current AGENTS.md, STATUS.md, active overrides, governance, contracts, schemas, validators, diagnostics, fixtures, tests, workflows, and open PRs. Classify the cause as run-only, documentation, contract, validator, sequence, negative-regression, conflicting-authority, or another evidence-backed cause. Stop for overlap or conflict.
[SOLUTION COMPARISON REQUIREMENT]
Identify at least two materially different repair options when credible alternatives exist; do not blindly implement this hypothesis.
[SELECTION CRITERIA]
Compare effectiveness, failure-detection timing, determinism, scope, compatibility, implementation cost, maintenance burden, AIGOV alignment, and overengineering risk. Select the smallest complete solution.
[BOUNDED IMPLEMENTATION AUTHORITY]
Only after Scope Gate is authorized, implement one focused branch. Do not merge or approve. Do not enable auto-merge, deploy, release, or modify repository settings.
[NON-GOALS]
No self-healing, automatic diagnosis, automatic repository repair, broad framework, or downstream redesign. Do not request hidden chain-of-thought.
[VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS]
Run targeted and relevant regression tests, negative cases, git diff --check, and applicable Schema/YAML/JSON checks. Report exact observed results; do not claim runtime enforcement from prose, fixtures, or CI alone.
[DRAFT PR REQUIREMENT]
Create one bounded Draft PR only after Scope and validation succeed.
[INDEPENDENT REVIEW REQUIREMENT]
Request a fresh independent exact-Head review. Any Head change makes prior review stale.
[FINAL RESPONSE FORMAT]
Return repository/base/head, Scope Gate, options, files, commands/results, Draft PR, enforcement achieved/not achieved, gaps, and merge_performed: false.
[STOP CONDITIONS]
Stop for unauthorized or insufficient Scope, conflicting authorities, equivalent open work, unknown identity, required broad framework/downstream change, or any authorization for the active Architect Run to edit its repository.
